text:
Skip to content
In UCI EdgeLab, we study and build distributed data management systems. We are interested in systems that span large geographic areas and infrastructures, including cloud and edge environments.
We target applications in the edge and IoT domain such as smart spaces, autonomous cars, and mobile applications.
WedgeDB
Wide-area Edge DB (WedgeDB)
is a distributed database that spans cloud and edge nodes. WedgeDB distributes storage and computes resources close to users to enable fast access. In EdgeLab, we tackle various research problems to realize WedgeDB. These research problems span topics such as consensus, byzantine agreement, transaction processing, and data indexing. Currently, the following are active projects within the WedgeDB scope.

Skip to content
The EdgeLab focuses on the research of moving processing from the cloud to the edge.
This topic follows recent industry trends to offload workloads from the cloud and to where data is generated. According to Gartner, by 2025, about 75% of the enterprise data will be generated and processed outside the data centers and the cloud (
source
).
However, companies realize that the Edge is a complicated environment. In particular, the Edge does not offer data services similar to what the cloud is able to offer.
Our Lab is researching this topic and collaborating with a company called
AnyLog
that is addressing the challenges that  companies are facing at the edge.
We published 2 papers with AnyLog:
AnyLog: a Grand Unification of the Internet of Things
in CIDR 2020
And a paper describing the implementation of the technology in CIDR 2024
The Tipping Point of Edge-Cloud Data Management
.
Our Lab is leveraging the AnyLog decentralized data management approach in the context of video management and AI. The adoption of AI requires efficient data processing systems. Video and AI bring huge efficiencies and value. For example, in traffic monitoring, self checkout in retail stores, security applications, and asset management (with drones). However, moving large volumes of video streams to the cloud is not economical and in many cases not feasible.
The lab will demonstrate data management of video streams at the edge (without centralizing the data) and the integration to AI at the Edge.
Here are some of the characteristics we plan to demonstrate:
Monitor the distributed edge data and resources from a single point – making the edge a cloud like environment.
Completely Decentralized architecture.
Data remains at the edge. No massive data transfers.
No Cloud cost/contracts.
Real-Time.
Data (the video streams and the inference AI data) remains in full control of the data owners.
Data is Secured – Using keys to sign messages and data.
Authentication and permission policies.
High Availability by replicating data between edge nodes
Using rule engines to make the edge nodes self-managed and extract insights and alerts from the data and AI insights.
The diagram below shows how data is managed to facilitate AI at the edge (without dependency on centralization):
At the collection point:
Data is first collected by sensors and cameras. We use AnyLog as the data platform that collects the data using a variety of APIs (like REST, MQTT, gRPC).
At the training point:
The training application queries the needed edge data as if the data is centralized. We use AnyLog to provide a virtual unified view of the distributed data (and the video images) that is queryable using SQL.
The training application generates a machine learning model based on the analyzed data and video.
At the execution point:
The model is pushed to the edge and executed. AnyLog provides a continuous stream of unified data to the model.
The model generates predictions which are hosted locally at the edge. AnyLog provides insights as if the distributed predictions are unified and centralized.
At the control point:
AnyLog rule engines evaluate the predictions and triggers alerts.
With the proper alerts, the behavior changes and impacts the data generated to create an updated model. This process continues repeatedly.
It is a unique process – it brings real-time, efficiency and cost savings and replaces long and tedious engineering effort with automation.

Four papers are published on IEEE blockchain 2022. Congratulations to Juncheng Fang, Farzad Habibi, Kevin C Bruhwiler, Fayzah Alshammari, Abhishek Singh, Yinan Zhou, Ahmad Showail, Hari Kishore Chaparala, Sai Vineeth Doddala, Samaa Gazzaz, Zane Karl, Hayden Freedman, Faisal Nawab.
08/30/2022

Selected Recent Publications
Reliable Transactions in Serverless-Edge Architecture
. By Suyash Gupta, Sajjad Rahnama, Erik Linsenmayer, Faisal Nawab, Mohammad Sadoghi. Published in IEEE ICDE 2023.
Challenges and future directions for Energy, Latency, and Lifetime Improvements in NVMs
. By S Kargar, F Nawab. Published in the Distributed and Parallel Databases (DAPD) journal 2022.
You’ve Got a Friend in ME (Mobile Edge): Blockchain Processing with Cloud Node Backup.
By Z Karl, H Freedman, A Singh, A Showail, S Gazzaz, F Nawab. Published in IEEE blockchain 2022.
LiftChain: A Scalable Multi-Stage NFT Transaction Protocol.
By H K Chaparala, S V Doddala, A Showail, A Singh, S Gazzaz, F Nawab. Published in IEEE blockchain 2022.
Analyzing Soft and Hard Partitions of Global-Scale Blockchain Systems.
By K C Bruhwiler, F Alshammari, F Habibi, J Fang, Y Zhou, A Singh, A Showail, F Nawab. Published in IEEE blockchain 2022.
PeloPartition: Improving Blockchain Resilience to Network Partitioning.
J Fang, F Habibi, K C Bruhwiler, F Alshammari, A Singh, Y Zhou, F Nawab. Published in IEEE blockchain 2022.
Efficient Dynamic Clustering: Capturing Patterns from Historical Cluster Evolution.
By B Gu, S Kargar, F Nawab. Published in EDBT 2022.
Croesus: Multi-Stage Processing and Transactions for Video-Analytics in Edge-Cloud Systems
. By S Gazzaz, V Chakraborty, F Nawab. Published in ICDE 2022.
Extending the lifetime of NVM: challenges and opportunities
. By S Kargar, F Nawab. Published in VLDB 2021.
Predict and Write: Using K-Means Clustering to Extend the Lifetime of NVM Storage.
By S Kargar, H Litz, F Nawab. Published in ICDE 2021.
CooLSM: Distributed and Cooperative Indexing Across Edge and Cloud Machines. By N Mittal, F Nawab. Published in ICDE 2021.
WedgeChain: A Trusted Edge-Cloud Store With Asynchronous (Lazy) Trust.
By F Nawab. Published in ICDE 2021.
AnyLog: a Grand Unification of the Internet of Things.
By D Abadi, O Arden, F Nawab, M Shadmon. Published in CIDR 2020.
Blockplane: A global-scale byzantizing middleware.
By F Nawab, M Sadoghi. Published in ICDE 2019.
Unifying consensus and atomic commitment for effective cloud data management.
By S Maiyya, F Nawab, D Agrawal, A El Abbadi. Published in VLDB 2019.
Blockchain in IoT Systems: End-to-End Delay Evaluation.
By M Alaslani, F Nawab, B Shihada. Published in IEEE Internet of Things Journal 2019.
Dpaxos: Managing data closer to users for low-latency and mobile applications.
By F Nawab, D Agrawal, A El Abbadi. Published in SIGMOD 2018.
